seoTitle: Generate a Report in HubSpot CRM: Builder Guide
metaDescription: Build custom HubSpot reports by choosing CRM data objects, dragging properties onto chart axes, and saving finished reports to a dashboard.
intro: HubSpot's custom report builder lets you turn raw CRM data into charts without needing a separate analytics tool. This guide covers picking your data object, whether that's Contacts, Deals, or Companies, and dragging the right properties onto your chart axes. It finishes with saving the report and adding it to a shared dashboard for ongoing tracking.

steps.heading: Open the Reports workspace
steps.body: Go to Reporting in HubSpot and click into the main Reports directory.

steps.heading: Start a custom report
steps.body: Click Create Report and choose the custom report builder instead of a pre-built template.

steps.heading: Pick your data object
steps.body: Select your target data structure, such as Contacts, Companies, or Deals, from the sidebar.

steps.heading: Assign properties to your axes
steps.body: Drag your chosen property fields onto the X and Y axis configurations.

steps.heading: Choose a chart type
steps.body: Switch between bar, line, pie, or table layouts depending on how you want to visualize the data.

steps.heading: Set filters and save
steps.body: Configure rolling date filters and criteria rules, then save the report and assign it to a dashboard.

faq.question: Can I build a report from multiple data objects?
faq.answer: HubSpot supports single-object and cross-object report building, depending on what your plan and data setup allow.

faq.question: What chart types are available in HubSpot reports?
faq.answer: You can choose from bar graphs, line charts, pie charts, or structured data tables.

faq.question: Can I add a rolling date range to my report?
faq.answer: Yes, the report builder supports sliding date ranges alongside specific criteria rule blocks.

faq.question: How do I get a report onto my dashboard?
faq.answer: After saving your report, you can assign it directly to an active dashboard from the save dialog.
